  "weights": {

  "active_set": ["R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Delegation is an unambiguous request for logical consistency checking, making this the sole load-bearing R1 dimension. The artifact contains two structurally critical contradictions: (1) the Data Collection and Detector sections establish a fully supervised pipeline — human annotators produce ground-truth labels y(x) used for training a binary classifier with class-balanced cross-entropy — while the Deployment Scope section asserts the system is 'fully unsupervised: it requires no annotated examples… and no ground-truth labels of any kind'; these claims are mutually exclusive and the contradiction is not reconciled anywhere in the text; (2) the Results section infers from AUC 0.91 that 'the rate of anomalous routing decisions in production multi-LLM systems is approximately 18 percent' — AUC is a rank-discrimination metric that is invariant to label prevalence and carries no information about base rates; the 18 % figure originates from the annotator-assigned labels, not from the detector's discriminative power, making the stated inferential chain a non sequitur."
  },
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": ""
}

rationale.R1.2: Delegation is an unambiguous request for logical consistency checking, making this the sole load-bearing R1 dimension. The artifact contains two structurally critical contradictions: (1) the Data Collection and Detector sections establish a fully supervised pipeline — human annotators produce ground-truth labels y(x) used for training a binary classifier with class-balanced cross-entropy — while the Deployment Scope section asserts the system is 'fully unsupervised: it requires no annotated examples… and no ground-truth labels of any kind'; these claims are mutually exclusive and the contradiction is not reconciled anywhere in the text; (2) the Results section infers from AUC 0.91 that 'the rate of anomalous routing decisions in production multi-LLM systems is approximately 18 percent' — AUC is a rank-discrimination metric that is invariant to label prevalence and carries no information about base rates; the 18 % figure originates from the annotator-assigned labels, not from the detector's discriminative power, making the stated inferential chain a non sequitur.
